## Chapter 14 Audit Checklist

The audit checklist provides a list of each step needed to complete the audit day. If desired,
print this table and use as a checklist while completing each task.


<table>
<tr>
<th colspan="2">Audit Checklist</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Audit Date:</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Audit Signature:</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</table>


<table>
<tr>
<th>Open Period</th>
<th>☐ Step 1</th>
<th>Open the current audit day.</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2"></td>
<td></td>
<td>Verify machine changes. · Verify machine adds and moves at this time.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>☐ Step 2</td>
<td>· Machine retires are verified just prior to closing the day. · Machine adds that have not had meters downloaded should be deferred to a later date.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Manual Meters</td>
<td>☐ Step 3</td>
<td>Enter manual meters. · If there are no manual meters to enter, cancel out of the Entry window, then click x in the upper-right corner of the screen. This completes the task (striking through the task and applying a black check mark).</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2">Correct Meter Variances</td>
<td></td>
<td>Investigate machine meter variances.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>☐ Step 4</td>
<td>· Export the current meters to Excel™ and calculate deltas, or have the system calculate corrections.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td>☐ Step 5</td>
<td>Adjust meters or accept variances. · After checking meter variances, adjust any meters that must be adjusted and accept variances that are correct.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Handpays</td>
<td>☐ Step 6</td>
<td>Balance jackpots and fills. · Balance jackpots and fills with actual hand-pay and fill slips by booth to the main cage.</td>
</tr>
</table>


<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="189" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 14 Audit Checklist" -->


<table>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td></td>
<td colspan="2">Audit Checklist</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td>☐ Step 7</td>
<td colspan="2">Add any manual hand pays. · If there are no manual hand pays, click Add Manual Hand Pays, cancel out of the entry screen, and then click Finish Manual Handpays in the top-right corner of the workbook. This will strike through the task and add a black check mark.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td>☐ Step 8</td>
<td colspan="2">Investigate jackpot, progressive, cancel credit, and bonus jackpot variances.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Voucher Drop</td>
<td>☐ Step 9</td>
<td colspan="2">Import voucher information. 1 Once the voucher drop is imported into Advantage, the Voucher In screen reflects the machines and voucher drop values.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2"></td>
<td rowspan="2">☐ Step 10</td>
<td colspan="2">Balance the total voucher drop to the soft count voucher report. · The total number of cash cans dropped should match the number of games dropped.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2">Ť Balance the Voucher In amount to the SC (Soft Count) report from EZ Pay. Balance the Voucher Out amount to the Issuance report from EZ Pay.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2"></td>
<td rowspan="2">☐ Step 11</td>
<td colspan="2">Investigate problems and variances for voucher drop. · Run the Machine Drop Report to see if variances wash from drop to drop.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2">· Investigate variances using the Machine Events tab in the Maintenance Workbook (777s). · Investigate variances using the Events screen. · Check variances for reasonableness by exporting the Meters screen to Excel™ and calculating deltas. · After checking meter variances, adjust variances that need to be adjusted and accept variances that are correct.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td>☐ Step 12</td>
<td colspan="2">Accept voucher variances.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Non-Cashable Electronic Promotion Drop</td>
<td>☐ Step 13</td>
<td colspan="2">Import NCEP information.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td>☐ Step 14</td>
<td colspan="2">Investigate problems and variances for NCEP drop. · Investigate variances using the Machine Events tab in the Maintenance Workbook (777s). · Investigate variances using the Events screen. · Check variances for reasonableness by exporting the Meters screen to Excel™ and calculating deltas. · After checking meter variances, adjust variances that need to be adjusted and accept variances that are correct.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td>☐ Step 15</td>
<td colspan="2">Accept NCEP variances.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td></td>
<td>OR</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</table>


<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="190" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 14 Audit Checklist" -->


<table>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th colspan="2">Audit Checklist</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>Cashable Electronic Promotion Drop</th>
<th>☐ Step 13</th>
<th>Import CEP information.</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td>☐ Step 14</td>
<td>Investigate problems and variances for CEP drop. · Investigate variances using the Machine Events tab in the Maintenance Workbook (777s). · Investigate variances using the Events screen. · Check variances for reasonableness by exporting the Meters screen to Excel™ and calculating deltas. · After checking meter variances, adjust variances that need to be adjusted and accept variances that are correct.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td>☐ Step 15</td>
<td>Accept CEP variances.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Soft Drop</td>
<td>☐ Step 16</td>
<td>Actual soft drop. · If there is no soft drop today, select No Drop Today from the Soft Drop Selection window.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="3"></td>
<td rowspan="3">☐ Step 17</td>
<td>Balance the total soft drop. · Match orphan cans to orphan machines. · The total number of cash cans dropped should match the number of games dropped.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>· Balance all dropped to the Soft Drop Summary report from the Drop Utility.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>O 1 The Soft Drop Summary report from the Drop Utility is normally printed in Soft Count and sent to Audit as part of their paperwork. This report can only be printed from the Drop Utility.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="3"></td>
<td rowspan="3">☐ Step 18</td>
<td>Investigate variances for soft drop. · Run the Machine Drop report to see if variances wash from drop to drop.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>· Investigate variances using the Machine Events tab in the Maintenance Workbook (777s). · Investigate variances and count individual bill inserts using the Events screen. · Check variances for reasonableness by exporting the Meters screen to Excel™ and calculating deltas.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>· After checking meter variances, adjust variances that need to be adjusted and accept variances that are correct.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2"></td>
<td rowspan="2">☐ Step 19</td>
<td>Audit test. · Use the Audit test to test the machine's soft meters, including voucher meters, to the system meters.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>O Ť The Audit test is not required to complete the daily audit and close the period. The Coin and Audit tests are only available through the View menu.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td>☐ Step 20</td>
<td>Print the Gaming Meter Audit Report for comparisons.</td>
</tr>
</table>


<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="191" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 14 Audit Checklist" -->


<table>
<tr>
<th colspan="2"></th>
<th>Audit Checklist</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="3">Close Period</td>
<td></td>
<td>Verify Machine Changes. · Verify machine retires at this time.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2">☐ Step 21</td>
<td>· A machine can be verified if final meters have not been downloaded but a final meter can be chosen.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>O 1 Retires occur at the end of the audit to ensure that the final drops, vouchers, and hand pays have been applied to the game.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2"></td>
<td rowspan="2">☐ Step 22</td>
<td>Print and balance reports.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>· Refer to Recommended Daily Reports on page 192 for more information.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2"></td>
<td rowspan="2">☐ Step 23</td>
<td>Compare the Metered Slot Win report, the Gaming Metered report, and the Machine Drop report. · Make sure the information in matching columns agree. Discrepancies could indicate:</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>· Previous periods are still open · Voucher drop occurred with no soft drop · Soft drop occurred with no voucher drop · Closing configuration is not set up correctly · Changes were made to data in a previous month</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td>☐ Step 24</td>
<td>Close Period · Click Close Period. The day closes.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td></td>
<td>☐ Step 25</td>
<td>Run the Metered Slot Win report, the Gaming Metered report, and the Machine Drop report again. · Verify the numbers after the period is closed.</td>
</tr>
</table>


### 14.1 Recommended Daily Reports

Use the following table to locate the reports recommended for completing the daily audit:


<table>
<tr>
<th>Report</th>
<th>Menu</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Actual Slot Win</td>
<td>Audit</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Adjustment</td>
<td>Audit</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Bill Acceptor Content</td>
<td>Slot Operations</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Bill Drop Variance</td>
<td>Audit</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Current Cash on Floor</td>
<td>Audit</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Drop Discrepancy</td>
<td>Audit</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Drop Reconciliation</td>
<td>Audit</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Gaming Metered Report</td>
<td>GCB</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Gaming Report</td>
<td>GCB</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Handpay (By fills, jackpots, overrides, and voids)</td>
<td>Audit</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Handpay for Period</td>
<td>Audit</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Hard and Soft Drop Variance Summary</td>
<td>Audit</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Hard Drop Variance</td>
<td>Audit</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Machine Drop (Includes vouchering)</td>
<td>GCB</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Machines Not Registered</td>
<td>System</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Meter Verification</td>
<td>Audit</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Metered Slot Win</td>
<td>Audit</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Metered Slot Win Lease</td>
<td>Audit</td>
</tr>
</table>
